name: Baked Blueberry Puff
recipeIngredient: nonstick cooking spray – butter flavor or regular
1 cup fresh blueberries, rinsed and patted dry
recipeInstructions:
NOTE: I did not use the 1 large egg it called for but used Egg Beaters instead.
Position a rack in the lower third of the oven and preheat to 425°F.
After preheating for 10 minutes, add the butter and a short spray (about 5 seconds) of cooking spray to the pan. Set the pan in the oven to preheat for another 10 minutes.
In a large bowl, whisk together the egg and egg white. Gradually whisk in the flour, milk, vanilla extract, sugar, nutmeg, and salt. Stir in the blueberries.
Set a small sifter over a dish and pour the confectioner's sugar into it. Set this and the halved lemon (plus a reamer if you have one) near the stove.
Pour the batter into the preheated pan and return it to the oven to bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the edges are puffed up high and golden brown.
Remove the pan from the oven. Squeeze the juice of the halved lemon over the entire pancake surface and sift on the confectioner's sugar. Serve hot, directly from the baking pan, cut into wedges. Spoon a little of the pan sauce over each slice.
Yield: One 10-inch pancake; 4 servings.
Advance Preparation:
The baking pan must be preheated in the oven for at least 15 minutes before adding the batter. The pancake should be served as soon as it is baked.
Temperature and Time:
425°F for 20 minutes to preheat the pan, plus 15 minutes to bake the pancake.
Although the recipe contains blueberries, you can make it with other berries or sliced fruit. Or you can make it plain without any berries, just lemon juice and sugar on top. Or as a dessert, you could prepare the pancake plain and serve it with a fruit compote and vanilla nonfat/lowfat/regular yogurt.
